steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes what a text corpus lets researchers test: “how _____ a word is in spoken and written English.” The example given — testing the incidence of “day” and finding it is the fifth most commonly used noun — makes clear the blank refers to how common or widespread a word is.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — assertive: Assertive means bold or forceful in manner. Words are not assertive in frequency data. ✗
  • Choice B — credible: Credible means believable or trustworthy. Corpus analysis doesn’t measure a word’s credibility. ✗
  • Choice C — pervasive: Pervasive means present or appearing everywhere — exactly what incidence and frequency rankings measure. ✓
  • Choice D — profound: Profound means having deep meaning. How meaningful a word is has no relation to how often it appears in a corpus. ✗
That matches Choice C — pervasive.
